That night, Mira Vale walked alone through the lower quarter of Port Saffron. She found the clerk from the Salt Guild in a tavern by the seawall, and she bought him a drink, and then another. By the third drink the clerk was explaining exactly which warehouse held the cargo of the Heron. Mira Vale thanked him, paid the keeper, and walked back along the seawall to where Elias Thorn waited with two lanterns and a crowbar. "Warehouse nine," Mira Vale said. Elias Thorn smiled for the first time in days.
